instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: (with content borrowed from wilkie and Vinicius Petrucci)

images.ai_description: Dark blue background with binary code pattern. Diagonal light blue and black stripes on the right. Upper right corner displays the number 310 in white.

input.title: What do I need to know now!

text_blocks.text: What do I need to know now!

runs.text: What do I need to know now!

text_blocks.text: You will be able to access the videos online

runs.text: You will be able to access the videos online

text_blocks.text: They are for your personal use only!
You don’t need to turn on your camera

runs.text: You don’t need to turn on your camera

text_blocks.text: If you do, you may be recorded

runs.text: If you do, you may be recorded

text_blocks.text: You can ask questions via text!

runs.text: You can ask questions via text!

text_blocks.text: Chat is great for that. If I don’t stop and read your questions, ask them again

runs.text: Chat is great for that. If I don’t stop and read your questions, ask them again

text_blocks.text: But feel free to interrupt me at any point.

runs.text: But feel free to interrupt me at any point.

text_blocks.text: I’m obligated to inform you that this is, in fact, a university course.

runs.text: I’m obligated to inform you that this is, in fact, a university course.

text_blocks.text: My name is Luis (pronounced Loo-eesh, but I don’t really care  )
I’m not from these parts as you can tell from my accent

runs.text: I’m not from these parts as you can tell from my accent

text_blocks.text: all the stuff I talk about today is on the course site

runs.text: all the stuff I talk about today is on the course site

text_blocks.text: office: 5421 SENSQ (haha – more like Zoom) (check site)
i before e except after c…
The ANSI C ProgrammingLanguage (2nd Edition)
By Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie

runs.text: By Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie

text_blocks.text: Conventions referred to as K&R style.

runs.text: Conventions referred to as K&R style.

text_blocks.text: Computer Systems:A Programmer’s Perspective (3rd Edition)
By Randal E. Bryant and David R. O’Hallaron

runs.text: By Randal E. Bryant and David R.

images.ai_description: Cover of "The C Programming Language, Second Edition" by Kernighan and Ritchie, featuring a prominent "C" with "ANSI" label, classic reference in computer programming.
The cover features a multicolored, abstract 3D surface representing data visualization, with the title "Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective" and authors Bryant and O'Hallaron.

text_blocks.text: Applied concepts and introduce tools and skills for lab-work.

runs.text: Applied concepts and introduce tools and skills for lab-work.

text_blocks.text: Provide deeper dive into some new skill or systems concept.

runs.text: Provide deeper dive into some new skill or systems concept.

text_blocks.text: You MUST WORK ALONE on all lab assignments.
Check due dates on the course website

runs.text: Check due dates on the course website

text_blocks.text: Many labs will assume the use of a specialized machine.

runs.text: Many labs will assume the use of a specialized machine.

text_blocks.text: Use your Pitt username and password.

runs.text: Use your Pitt username and password.

text_blocks.text: Talk to your TA if you have any issues. (Do NOT start assignments late!)

runs.text: Talk to your TA if you have any issues. (Do NOT start assignments late!)

text_blocks.text: Covers most normal setbacks and life and schedule mishaps.

runs.text: Covers most normal setbacks and life and schedule mishaps.

text_blocks.text: A maximum of 2 Late Days per lab assignment.
That is, assignments 3 days late will always take at least 1 penalty day.

runs.text: That is, assignments 3 days late will always take at least 1 penalty day.

text_blocks.text: Late penalty incurs a 15% penalty for each day. (out of original 100%)
An assignment cannot be submitted after the 3rd penalty day.
Major emergencies require haste communication with me and your advisor.

runs.text: Major emergencies require haste communication with me and your advisor.

text_blocks.text: I don’t keep track of attendance

runs.text: I don’t keep track of attendance

text_blocks.text: But you should come to class!

runs.text: But you should come to class!

text_blocks.text: A lot of the concepts are best demonstrated interactively.

runs.text: A lot of the concepts are best demonstrated interactively.

text_blocks.text: You CANNOT pass without doing the lab assignments.

runs.text: pass without doing the lab assignments.

text_blocks.text: They will email me, and I will listen to what they tell me to do.

runs.text: They will email me, and I will listen to what they tell me to do.

text_blocks.text: The default penalty is to be removed from the course with a failing grade

runs.text: The default penalty is to be removed from the course with a failing grade

text_blocks.text: Pro-tip: DON’T CHEAT. Start early. Ask appropriate staff for help.

runs.text: . Start early. Ask appropriate staff for help.

text_blocks.text: The syllabus online has a more thorough policy.

runs.text: The syllabus online has a more thorough policy.

text_blocks.text: Jokes/comments about sex, gender, race, ethnicity, religion, etc are not tolerated. Includes any online spaces involved.

runs.text: Jokes/comments about sex, gender, race, ethnicity, religion,
are not tolerated. Includes any online spaces involved.

text_blocks.text: I’m not grading lab assignments, but I still look at your work.

runs.text: I’m not grading lab assignments, but I still look at your work.

text_blocks.text: Ask for help (There are PLENTY of resources)

runs.text: Ask for help (There are PLENTY of resources)

text_blocks.text: TAs and my own office hours

runs.text: TAs and my own office hours

text_blocks.text: I can definitely tell when someone cheats.

runs.text: I can definitely tell when someone cheats.

text_blocks.text: The University is justifiably strict about it.

runs.text: The University is justifiably strict about it.

text_blocks.text: Do not publish your code until after the semester (if at all)

runs.text: Do not publish your code until

text_blocks.text: I want you to walk away with a direction/goal to do something else.

runs.text: I want you to walk away with a direction/goal to do something else.

text_blocks.text: Hopefully, you find something to be inspired by.

runs.text: Hopefully, you find something to be inspired by.

text_blocks.text: I trust my students that they could learn on their own.

runs.text: I trust my students that they could learn on their own.

text_blocks.text: But don’t want them to have to do so.
Ask questions!  Challenge concepts!  Ask for help!

runs.text: Ask questions!  Challenge concepts!  Ask for help!

text_blocks.text: You ARE learning! You are not supposed to know things…

runs.text: You ARE learning! You are not supposed to know things…

text_blocks.text: Even if you are supposed to know that, you don’t… so ask!

runs.text: Even if you are supposed to know that, you don’t… so ask!

text_blocks.text: Don’t be scared! I like to help!

runs.text: Don’t be scared! I like to help!

text_blocks.text: Come to lectures synchronously if you can!

runs.text: Come to lectures synchronously if you can!

text_blocks.text: You have access to me, we can interact

runs.text: You have access to me, we can interact

text_blocks.text: You can ask questions, and get the answers promptly!

runs.text: You can ask questions, and get the answers promptly!

text_blocks.text: (D) I completed CS 447 at least 2 semesters ago

runs.text: (D) I completed CS 447 at least 2 semesters ago

images.ai_description: A comic depicts a remote worker celebrating the end of the workday by simply rotating their chair, humorously illustrating the blur between work and home.

text_blocks.text: What were your experiences last semester with remote teaching?

runs.text: What were your experiences last semester with remote teaching?

text_blocks.text: If the university authorizes, do you want to attend classes physically?

runs.text: If the university authorizes, do you

text_blocks.text: Why? (I really want to know your reasons!)

runs.text: Why? (I really want to know your reasons!)

text_blocks.text: Note the question is not do you prefer  (cause, ya know, we all preferred no CoViD pandemic)

runs.text: Note the question is not do you

text_blocks.text: My opinion: There is no benefit in coming that overweighs your health.

runs.text: : There is no benefit in coming that overweighs your health.

text_blocks.text: How should I distribute my office hours?

runs.text: How should I distribute my office hours?

text_blocks.text: Lectures will be synchronous! And online! ALWAYS!

runs.text: Lectures will be synchronous! And online! ALWAYS!

text_blocks.text: Have a job? some scheduling problem? Idk?

runs.text: Have a job? some scheduling problem? Idk?

text_blocks.text: IF we go back to the classroom

runs.text: IF we go back to the classroom

text_blocks.text: First lecture, I’ll be in the classroom, you will be at home. Why?

runs.text: First lecture, I’ll be in the classroom, you will be at home. Why?

text_blocks.text: If technical issues come, I’ll just relocate to teach remotely

runs.text: If technical issues come, I’ll just relocate to teach remotely

text_blocks.text: You can experience how it feels (teacher in classroom, students remote)

runs.text: You can experience how it feels (teacher in classroom, students remote)

text_blocks.text: EVERYONE WILL FOLLOW THE RULES (distance, mask, etc.!)

runs.text: EVERYONE WILL FOLLOW THE RULES (distance, mask, etc.!)

text_blocks.text: If food were knowledge, this would be, like, our restaurant menu.

runs.text: If food were knowledge, this would be, like, our restaurant menu.

text_blocks.text: We’re going to (tentatively) learn SO MUCH fun stuff!
We gain an appreciation of abstraction (and respecting limitations)

runs.text: We gain an appreciation of abstraction (and respecting limitations)

text_blocks.text: Learning the “why” for many systems abstractions

runs.text: Learning the “why” for many systems abstractions

text_blocks.text: Thinking about how systems might change in the future

runs.text: Thinking about how systems might change in the future

text_blocks.text: We demystify software so as to no longer be a hostage to its design

runs.text: We demystify software so as to no longer be a hostage to its design

text_blocks.text: A subfield of CS dealing with the interactions between software/hardware.

runs.text: A subfield of CS dealing with the interactions between software/hardware.

text_blocks.text: A layer that provides abstractions and must constantly reevaluate them.

runs.text: A layer that provides abstractions and must constantly reevaluate them.

text_blocks.text: A house built from trade-offs in approach…

runs.text: A house built from trade-offs in approach…

text_blocks.text: Do you build better hardware? Add more memory?

runs.text: Do you build better hardware? Add more memory?

text_blocks.text: Or, do you design better software?

runs.text: Or, do you design better software?

text_blocks.text: Do you choose the specialized path?

runs.text: Do you choose the specialized path?

text_blocks.text: Or, do you create a general system?

runs.text: Or, do you create a general system?

text_blocks.text: Looking for guidance by looking at recent research:

runs.text: Looking for guidance by looking at recent research:

text_blocks.text: SOSP/OSDI/EuroSys – OS design, kernel design, virtualization

runs.text: File systems unfit as distributed storage backends: lessons from 10 years of

runs.text: Machine Learning Systems are Stuck in a Rut

runs.text: I/O Is Faster Than the CPU -- Let's Partition Resources and Eliminate (Most) OS Abstractions

text_blocks.text: I'm Not Dead Yet!: The Role of the Operating System in a Kernel-Bypass Era

runs.text: I'm Not Dead Yet!: The Role of the Operating System in a Kernel-Bypass Era

text_blocks.text: Unikernels: The Next Stage of Linux's Dominance

runs.text: Unikernels: The Next Stage of Linux's Dominance

text_blocks.text: Because B sucks and D wasn’t invented yet. J/K.

runs.text: Because B sucks and D wasn’t invented yet. J/K.

text_blocks.text: C was invented in 1972 alongside UNIX to an effort to aid application development of that system.

runs.text: C was invented in 1972 alongside UNIX to an effort to aid application development of that system.

text_blocks.text: Eventually UNIX itself was rewritten in C cementing C as a systems language.

runs.text: Eventually UNIX itself was rewritten in C cementing C as a systems language.

text_blocks.text: As such, C provides a high-level abstraction of assembly / machine-code and a low-level abstraction of memory, from the perspective of the C programmer.

runs.text: As such, C provides a high-level abstraction of assembly / machine-code and a low-level abstraction of memory, from the perspective of the C programmer.

text_blocks.text: This is important for programming systems code!

runs.text: This is important for programming systems code!

text_blocks.text: Allows full manipulation of memory (to one’s peril, often.)

runs.text: Allows full manipulation of memory (to one’s peril, often.)

text_blocks.text: This, in turn, allows for full manipulation of cpu/hardware.

runs.text: This, in turn, allows for full manipulation of

text_blocks.text: Learning C helps you understand Systems.

runs.text: Learning C helps you understand Systems.

text_blocks.text: Understanding Systems lets youmake them better.
C reveals the underlying memorymodel and execution environment.
Even if you do not have the original code.

runs.text: Even if you do not have the original code.

text_blocks.text: Failing at C helps you learn…

runs.text: Failing at C helps you learn…

text_blocks.text: Because then you debug your program.

runs.text: Because then you debug your program.

runs.text: And debuggers are very useful tools.

runs.text: Important that your abstractions don’t hurt performance because EVERY user application suffers.
